title: runtime-resolver.ts trust-boundary logic is fully mocked out, never executed under test
evidence: runtime-resolver.ts implements the anti-tampering identity check that binds the Pi CLI entry to its owning package (owningPackageRoot symlink/traversal containment via `inside()`, package-name spoofing rejection, declared-bin-vs-canonical-anchor match, before/after re-resolution match in loadPiRuntime) and fail-closes via `fail()` throwing PI_RUNTIME_DIAGNOSIS on any mismatch. The only test file that references this module, `runner-isolation.test.ts:49-51`, mocks it out entirely: `vi.mock("../src/runtime-resolver.ts", ... resolvePiRuntimeIdentity: runnerMocks.resolveRuntimeIdentity)`. No test imports the unmocked module or calls resolvePiRuntimeIdentity/loadPiRuntime/owningPackageRoot directly.
impact: The exact security-critical logic guarding which Pi package is trusted as the runtime (path containment, name/version match, bin-target verification, before/after re-check against a swapped runtime mid-load) is asserted nowhere; a regression that weakens `inside()` or the identitiesMatch re-check would only surface in production, not CI.
recommendation: Add direct unit tests for runtime-resolver.ts exercising: a package.json outside the anchor's containment (rejected), wrong package name (rejected), bin target pointing outside packageRoot (rejected), export target escaping packageRoot (rejected), and a mid-load swap causing the after-import identity check in loadPiRuntime to fail closed.
acceptance_criteria: A test imports the real (unmocked) runtime-resolver.ts and exercises resolvePiRuntimeIdentity's fail-closed branches
loadPiRuntime's before/after identity-match re-check is covered by a test that swaps the runtime mid-load
